summary.intent: Fix review findings from an automated Codex review pass on PR #669 in fogodev/ars-ui, covering a behavior bug in FilterMode::Custom and two spec sync issues related to public API/label changes.
summary.outcome: Three Codex review threads were resolved: the Custom filter mode highlight bug was fixed (highlight now left empty on raw input changes when filtering is adapter-owned), and two spec files were updated to match the closure-free Custom variant and the new listbox_label message. Changes were committed as 464d4dbf (earlier pass) and a subsequent commit, CI passed, and the PR was advanced.

repo: The repo uses an automated Codex reviewer bot that signals state via GitHub reactions (eyes = reviewing, +1 = approved) and PR review threads; polling both is necessary to detect terminal state.

CI runs in two waves: first wave covers formatting, clippy, workspace check, mutual exclusion, snapshot change check, and unit tests; second wave covers coverage, release verification, adapter parity, and feature-flag jobs.
Spec conformance is validated via `cargo xtask spec validate`, which must pass alongside unit tests and clippy.
The combobox FilterMode::Custom variant is closure-free; the spec and adapter harness must reflect this.
The listbox aria-label uses a dedicated localized `listbox_label` message rather than an unconditional `aria-labelledby` reference pointing at a potentially-omitted label element.
SyncProps is scoped to only preserve or close an already-open popup; it must not passively reopen a user-dismissed filtered combobox on unrelated prop changes.

code.finding: FilterMode::Custom handling; highlight must be left empty on raw input changes when filtering is adapter-owned to avoid stale highlight state.
SyncProps logic for popup open/close state; must not reopen a user-closed filtered combobox when unrelated props change.
custom_filter_mode function region; patched as part of the custom highlight fix.
Spec section describing FilterMode::Custom and listbox label behavior; updated to match closure-free Custom variant and listbox_label message.
Adapter harness spec for Custom filter mode; updated to reflect closure-free Custom variant.

workflow: Polling Codex state requires checking both the reactions API and the review threads GraphQL endpoint; relying on only one can miss the terminal signal.
Defensive re-checks of reactions and threads are useful when the polling loop has been running for an extended period (~10 min) without a terminal signal.
Resolving Codex threads requires both posting a reply comment (referencing the fix commit) and calling the resolveReviewThread GraphQL mutation explicitly.

After pushing a fix, triggering a new `@codex review` pass with a PR comment restarts the review cycle; verifying zero unresolved Codex threads before triggering avoids re-reviewing already-resolved issues.
`cargo llvm-cov test` output is piped to a temp file and tailed to avoid truncation in terminal output.

friction: Codex review cycle is slow (~10+ minutes per pass), requiring extended polling loops with 60-second sleep intervals.
Determining Codex terminal state requires correlating two separate API endpoints (reactions and review threads), which adds complexity to the polling logic.
The two-wave CI structure means full validation feedback is delayed; some checks (coverage, adapter parity) only run after the first wave completes.
open_items: No explicitly deferred items were mentioned; all three Codex findings from this pass were addressed and resolved.
A subsequent Codex review pass was triggered after the fix commit — its verdict is pending and may produce additional findings.
